official.name: Elise M. Stefanik
official.office_address: 2211 Rayburn House Office Building Washington DC 20515-3221

summary.narrative: On her 2014 campaign platform, Stefanik's healthcare pledges (ACA repeal/replace, cross-state insurance, tort reform, high-risk pools) show little to no documented delivery in the supplied record, with verification anchored only to the original platform text. Her district-facing commitments fared better in documented activity: she repeatedly advocated for Fort Drum and military families and sponsored numerous military and agriculture bills, though most remained at the introduction or advocacy stage rather than enactment. Negative/values-type pledges on Common Core and taxes are partly supported by her voting record but lack Tier-1 verification specific to this term.
summary.grade_rationale: The record pairs broadly stated campaign pledges with mostly early-stage delivery: consistent advocacy and bill introductions on defense and agriculture, but few documented enacted outcomes, and several healthcare platform items with no documented action in the supplied evidence. Promise quality is mostly directional-to-specific (clarity 2-4) with limited measurable targets, and her causal role is largely supporting given the multi-actor legislative environment.

data_gaps: Campaign-window dating is weak: most platform items (c1-c17) are undated or dated to election day with no campaign-window confirmation; coverage notes zero campaign-window-dated candidates. Healthcare promises lack Tier-1 delivery verification, so D-scores are low-confidence and several rest on absence of evidence. The supplied verification set is dominated by bill-introduction records (mostly 'Referred to Committee'), few enactments shown; only c232 and a couple of postal/coin bills became law and are not tied to these promises. Tax pledge is compound and contested (c287 reports she voted against the 2017 tax cuts). Human reviewer should confirm campaign-era attribution and seek Tier-1 verification of healthcare and tax votes within the 2015-2027 term.

provisional_status.display: Grade is record-to-date — term in progress, 95.5% elapsed (as of 2026-06-22). It will change as the term continues.
provisional_status.note: Distinct from any per-promise 'PROVISIONAL (early-term)' flag in scorecard.card_flags, which concerns a single promise's own deadline, not the term's completeness.
